description_long: This is a small, lifelike bird with a compact body, short neck, and a slightly rounded head. Its plumage is predominantly brown with subtle lighter streaks and mottling along the wings and flanks, giving it a natural, camouflaged appearance. The beak is short, conical, and pointed, well-suited for seed eating. The tail is straight and moderately long, tapering to a rounded tip, and the legs are slender and appear to be a muted grayish color. The surface texture is matte with a slight sheen, resembling the soft, fine feathers of a real bird. This bird would be commonly seen perched or foraging in urban parks, gardens, or on sidewalks.
description: A small, brown bird with subtle streaked plumage, a conical beak, and a rounded tail. Its lifelike details and natural coloration make it well-suited for urban environments.
description_view_0: Side view showing the bird's compact body, streaked brown plumage, and straight tail.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the rounded head, symmetrical wings, and the full length of the tail.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same body shape and plumage pattern as view 0, with the beak and tail clearly visible.
description_view_3: Frontal view showing the bird's head, beak, and chest, with both wings tucked close to the body.
attribute_plumage_pattern: mottled and streaked with lighter and darker brown tones across the wings and body
